purpose: Milestones 1–8 source/build runtime-ownership evidence for Octane's DOM-free Lynx graphs, including the dual-layer first-screen specialization and lazy chunks. This is JavaScript graph, decoded-bundle, and host-emulator evidence, not Lynx Web or native device execution.

sources.evidence: Lynx documents PrimJS on the main thread, PrimJS by default on Android background threads, JavaScriptCore by default on iOS background threads, ES2019/ES2015 syntax ceilings, and the Map/Set/WeakMap/WeakSet, Array.flatMap, Object.entries/values, Promise.finally, and Symbol.prototype.description built-ins used by the universal core.
Lynx 3.2 introduced Promise microtask semantics and lynx.queueMicrotask; the selected SDK is 3.9.0.
Lynx documents the globalThis object of its dual-thread environment; the universal core uses it only to resolve the optional standard queueMicrotask global.
The exact Rspeedy 0.16.0 package records an ES2017 default output target and explicit block-scoping lowering for both layers.

main-thread.entry: packages/rspeedy-plugin-octane/tests/_fixtures/application/src/background.ts (preceded and followed by generated receiver/sync modules)
main-thread.graphContract: Evaluates the same authored entry as the background layer after installing @octanejs/lynx/main-thread. The exact @octanejs/lynx package-root import resolves to @octanejs/lynx/first-screen, compiled components use @octanejs/lynx/main-renderer, and the graph contains the receiver, typed Engine data-lifecycle snapshot bridge, Element PAPI adapter, first-tree journal, Lynx-specific list host, and host prop/event boundary without the universal core, background client driver, or background root.
main-thread.nativeExecution: blocked by the Phase 0 Explorer/Android/iOS gates

background.graphContract: Evaluates the same authored entry as the main layer through the public Lynx root and contains the page-lifetime data receiver, client driver, transport, shared host-attachment protocol, host-prop/event/NodesRef client modules, and Octane universal native core without the main-thread host, first-screen renderer, or list PAPI code.
background.platformSubpathContract: @octanejs/lynx/platform is a separate background-only authoring entry. Source tests cover init/global snapshot hooks, public GlobalEventEmitter subscriptions, typed Native Modules, reload request forwarding, and error reporting; compiler tests reject its import in main-thread specialization. The default root graph now owns only the internal page-lifetime data application seam used by those stores, not the public platform-hook exports.
background.nativeExecution: blocked by the Phase 0 Explorer/Android/iOS gates

mainThreadRenderOnlyContract.sync: The generated receiver opens background readiness only after the authored entry's synchronous initialization returns.
mainThreadRenderOnlyContract.ownership: The main renderer emits one initial host batch and event markers. Effects, refs, state ownership, listener closures, scheduling, and later updates remain background-owned.
mainThreadRenderOnlyContract.handoff: A clone-safe snapshot is paired with a main-local host journal. Compatible background batches transfer the existing host tree without allocation or structural mutation; ordinary events replay in FIFO order after background listener ownership; typed mismatches repair from the background tree.
mainThreadRenderOnlyContract.excluded: Native list hosts and materializations are not captured for adoption. Boolean list defer has no eager-main versus deferred-background claim. A first-screen lazy pending arm is one-shot; the background root owns later reveal or rejection.
mainThreadRenderOnlyContract.evidence: Compiler, source-graph, decoded-bundle, and official JavaScript host-emulator tests only; no native first-paint, identity, device, or performance evidence.

universalCoreBuiltins.microtaskContract: Universal roots use the standard queueMicrotask global when present. A native host without that global must pass options.scheduleMicrotask; Lynx provides lynx.queueMicrotask for this integration point.
universalCoreBuiltins.symbolContract: Nested hook slots use Symbol.prototype.description, which the selected Lynx runtimes document as supported.

qualification: The exact pinned Rspeedy toolchain specializes one authored Milestone 8 application entry into production background and main-thread graphs and emits decoded entry and content-hashed async .lynx.bundle artifacts. The lazy module is present in both Octane layers. The background graph contains the full universal runtime, page-lifetime data receiver, and asynchronous client boundary; the main graph contains the one-shot first-screen renderer, typed Engine data bridge, receiver, and PAPI host while excluding the universal core. Both graphs exclude DOM, React, Preact, and ReactLynx runtime modules. Source and JavaScript-host tests cover snapshot adoption, retained boundaries, same-root portals, event handoff, data-lifecycle updates, mismatch repair, and cleanup. This does not prove main-thread bytecode execution, native first paint, native identity retention, native chunk execution or portal placement, native event/data-lifecycle context or delivery, list allocation/scrolling, Lynx Web, Android, iOS, or performance; every corresponding formal gate remains blocked.
